Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mounds located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: * Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) * Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=qq.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun Kofun: * Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) * Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: * Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) * Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: * Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) * Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=qq.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: * Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) * Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! Notes |- | '''[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]'''|| 34°34′11.36″N 135°38′43.68″E || Zenpokoen-fun || Kofun length: 130 m || 1877, 1955, 1985-1986 || National Historic Site; rich burial artifacts, including haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{citation needed|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|12=draft}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=draft}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|12=draft}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=draft}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! Notes |- | '''[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]'''|| 34°34′11.36″N 135°38′43.68″E || Zenpokoen-fun || Kofun length: 130 m || 1877, 1955, 1985-1986 || National Historic Site; rich burial artifacts, including haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)|12=draft}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{citation needed|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[en:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:atsuokayama Kofun Group]]

|— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{citation needed|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[en:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:atsuokayama Kofun Group]]

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:松岳山古墳群 up to rev 72082786
revision.text:
{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=松岳山
古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|松岳山古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''松岳山古墳群'''（まつおかやまこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]国分市場にある[[古墳群]]。1基が国の[[史跡]]に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]・柏原市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[松岳山古墳]]}}]] 大阪府東部、[[大和川]]左岸の松岳山丘陵上に営造された古墳群である。中心的古墳である[[松岳山古墳]]など9基以上の古墳で構成される。松岳山古墳・茶臼塚古墳以外の古墳は宅地造成により消滅しており、内容の詳細は明らかでない。 古墳群のうち松岳山古墳は大型前方後円墳（唯一の前方後円墳）であるが、その他の古墳は小規模な[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]になる。松岳山丘陵の西から東へと営造され、築造に際しては大量の安山岩・玄武岩の板石が使用される。特に松岳山古墳は板石積み・石棺型式・立石・楕円形円筒埴輪の点で全国的にも特異な様相を示す古墳であり、その他の古墳も小規模ながら豊富な副葬品を伴う点で注目される{{Sfn|松岳山古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 この松岳山古墳群は、古墳時代前期前葉-後葉頃の[[3世紀]]末-[[4世紀]]後半頃の営造と推定される。柏原市域では南西の玉手山丘陵においても同時期の大型前方後円墳群として[[玉手山古墳群]]の営造が知られるが、両古墳群の内容は大きく相違するため、同古墳群とともに古墳時代中期のヤマト大王墓（[[古市古墳群]]）の営造以前の河内地方の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳群になる。また[[江戸時代]]には松岳山から[[船氏王後墓誌]]（[[国宝]]）が出土したといい、渡来系氏族の船氏との関連を指摘する説もある。 古墳群のうち松岳山古墳の古墳域は国の[[史跡]]に指定されているほか、多くの出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]・柏原市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+松岳山古墳群の一覧[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ 松岳山古墳群]（柏原市ホームページ、2016年10月25日更新版）。[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400026/?doc_id=5198 松岳山古墳群一覧表]（柏原市ホームページ、2016年10月23日更新版）。 !古墳名 !座標 !形状 !規模 !調査 !備考 |- |'''[[松岳山古墳]]'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|11.36|N|135|38|43.68|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=松岳山古墳}}||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長130m||1877年
1955年
1985-1986年||style="text-align:left"|国の史跡（後円部）
副葬品出土
出土埴輪・土器は柏原市指定有形文化財 |- |'''向井山茶臼塚古墳'''
（茶臼山古墳）||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|10.84|N|135|38|37.23|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=向井山茶臼塚古墳（消滅）}}||[[円墳]]?||直径約20m||1629年発見||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳
副葬品出土
出土銅鏡3面は国の重要文化財 |- |'''市場茶臼塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|10.87|N|135|38|35.65|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=市場茶臼塚古墳（消滅）}}||円墳?||直径約10m|| ||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳 |- |'''[[茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)|茶臼塚古墳]]'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|10.23|N|135|38|39.97|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=茶臼塚古墳}}||長方形墳||16m×22m||1984-1985年||style="text-align:left"|副葬品出土
出土品は柏原市指定有形文化財 |- |'''ヌク谷北塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|12.40|N|135|38|46.29|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷北塚古墳（消滅）}}|| || ||1961年||style="text-align:left"|副葬品出土 |- |'''ヌク谷南塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|11.72|N|135|38|46.50|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷南塚古墳（消滅）}}||円墳||直径11m||1877年||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳
副葬品出土 |- |'''ヌク谷東ノ大塚古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|12.24|N|135|38|47.23|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷東ノ大塚古墳（消滅）}}||円墳||直径30m||1877年||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳
副葬品出土
出土歯車形碧玉製品は国の重要文化財 |- |'''ヌク谷芝山西古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|14.08|N|135|38|48.82|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷芝山西古墳（消滅）}}||円墳?|| ||なし||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳 |- |'''ヌク谷芝山東古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|14.17|N|135|38|50.95|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷芝山東古墳（消滅）}}||円墳?|| ||なし||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳 |} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品（考古資料） *: 向井山茶臼塚古墳出土品。明細は以下。所有者は国分神社、[[大阪市立美術館]]保管。1953年（昭和28年）11月14日指定、1954年（昭和29年）9月12日に追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9724|河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200324/?doc_id=1523 河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 ** 青蓋盤竜鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 2面 * 歯車形碧玉製品（考古資料） *: ヌク谷東ノ大塚古墳出土。藤田美術館保管。1954年（昭和29年）3月20日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9733|歯車形碧玉製品}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082300017/?doc_id=1535 歯車形碧玉製品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 === 国の史跡 === * 松岳山古墳 - 1922年（大正11年）3月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1770|松岳山古墳}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082300055/?doc_id=1539 松岳山古墳]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 === 柏原市指定文化財 === {{Double image aside|right|松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|100|松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-2.JPG|105|{{Center|松岳山古墳出土 楕円筒埴輪}}{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示。}}}} [[ファイル:茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 三角縁神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|茶臼塚古墳出土
三角縁神獣鏡}}{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示。}}]] * 有形文化財 ** 松岳山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）8月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 松岳山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ、2014年8月29日更新版）。。 *** 楕円筒埴輪 3点 *** 壺形土器 2点 ** 茶臼塚古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）8月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000078/?doc_id=1455 茶臼塚古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ、2014年8月29日更新版）。。 *** 三角縁神獣鏡 1点 *** 四獣鏡 1点 *** 鍬形石 6点 *** 車輪石 8点 *** 石釧 41点 *** 刀子 2点 *** 鉄斧 1点 *** 鎌 1点 *** 鏃 2点 *** 円筒埴輪 1点 === 関連文化財 === * [[船氏王後墓誌|銅製船氏王後墓誌]] *: [[国宝]]（考古資料）。伝松岳山出土であるが経緯不明。[[三井記念美術館]]保管。1953年（昭和28年）3月31日に国の重要文化財に指定、1961年（昭和36年）4月27日に国宝に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|859|銅製船氏王後墓誌}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200263/?doc_id=1517 銅製船氏王後墓誌]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松岳山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松岳山古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[玉手山古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ 松岳山古墳群] - 柏原市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:まつおかやまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

出土歯車形碧玉製品は国の重要文化財 |- |'''ヌク谷芝山西古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|14.08|N|135|38|48.82|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷芝山西古墳（消滅）}}||円墳?|| ||なし||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳 |- |'''ヌク谷芝山東古墳'''||{{ウィキ座標|34|34|14.17|N|135|38|50.95|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|位置|name=ヌク谷芝山東古墳（消滅）}}||円墳?|| ||なし||style="text-align:left"|消滅古墳 |} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品（考古資料） *: 向井山茶臼塚古墳出土品。明細は以下。所有者は国分神社、[[大阪市立美術館]]保管。1953年（昭和28年）11月14日指定、1954年（昭和29年）9月12日に追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9724|河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200324/?doc_id=1523 河内国南河内郡茶臼山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 ** 青蓋盤竜鏡 1面 ** 三角縁神獣鏡 2面 * 歯車形碧玉製品（考古資料） *: ヌク谷東ノ大塚古墳出土。藤田美術館保管。1954年（昭和29年）3月20日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9733|歯車形碧玉製品}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082300017/?doc_id=1535 歯車形碧玉製品]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 === 国の史跡 === * 松岳山古墳 - 1922年（大正11年）3月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1770|松岳山古墳}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082300055/?doc_id=1539 松岳山古墳]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 === 柏原市指定文化財 === {{Double image aside|right|松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-1.JPG|100|松岳山古墳出土 鰭付楕円形円筒埴輪-2.JPG|105|{{Center|松岳山古墳出土 楕円筒埴輪}}{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示。}}}} [[ファイル:茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 三角縁神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|160px|right|{{center|茶臼塚古墳出土
三角縁神獣鏡}}{{small|柏原市立歴史資料館展示。}}]] * 有形文化財 ** 松岳山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）8月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000061/?doc_id=1454 松岳山古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ、2014年8月29日更新版）。。 *** 楕円筒埴輪 3点 *** 壺形土器 2点 ** 茶臼塚古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。柏原市立歴史資料館保管。2008年（平成20年）8月21日指定[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082000078/?doc_id=1455 茶臼塚古墳出土品]（柏原市ホームページ、2014年8月29日更新版）。。 *** 三角縁神獣鏡 1点 *** 四獣鏡 1点 *** 鍬形石 6点 *** 車輪石 8点 *** 石釧 41点 *** 刀子 2点 *** 鉄斧 1点 *** 鎌 1点 *** 鏃 2点 *** 円筒埴輪 1点 === 関連文化財 === * [[船氏王後墓誌|銅製船氏王後墓誌]] *: [[国宝]]（考古資料）。伝松岳山出土であるが経緯不明。[[三井記念美術館]]保管。1953年（昭和28年）3月31日に国の重要文化財に指定、1961年（昭和36年）4月27日に国宝に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|859|銅製船氏王後墓誌}}[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200263/?doc_id=1517 銅製船氏王後墓誌]（柏原市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=松岳山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|松岳山古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[玉手山古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ 松岳山古墳群] - 柏原市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:まつおかやまこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! Notes |- | '''[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]'''|| 34°34′11.36″N 135°38′43.68″E || Zenpokoen-fun || Kofun length: 130 m || 1877, 1955, 1985-1986 || National Historic Site; rich burial artifacts, including haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{citation needed|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[en:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:atsuokayama Kofun Group]] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! Notes |- | '''[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]'''|| 34°34′11.36″N 135°38′43.68″E || Zenpokoen-fun || Kofun length: 130 m || 1877, 1955, 1985-1986 || National Historic Site; rich burial artifacts, including haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|qq=}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwara]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[en:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:atsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]]

{{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|qid=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|qid=Q11530088}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60986182]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! Notes |- | '''[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]'''|| 34°34′11.36″N 135°38′43.68″E || Zenpokoen-fun || Kofun length: 130 m || 1877, 1955, 1985-1986 || National Historic Site; rich burial artifacts, including haniwa and pottery (Kashiwara City Cultural Assets). |- | '''Mukaimachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.84″N 135°38′37.23″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~20 m || Discovered in 1629 || Destroyed; artifacts include three bronze mirrors designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- | '''Ichibachausuyama Kofun'''|| 34°34′10.87″N 135°38′35.65″E || Empun? || Diameter: ~10 m || —|| Destroyed. |- | {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)|qq=}}|| 34°34′10.23″N 135°38′39.97″E || Rectangular || 16 × 22 m || 1984-1985 || Burial artifacts, including tools and mirrors, are designated Kashiwara City Cultural Assets. |- |'''Nukudani North Kofun''' |34°34′12.40″N 135°38′46.29″E |Unknown |— |1961 |Excavated; burial artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani South Kofun''' |34°34′11.72″N 135°38′46.50″E |Empun |Diameter: 11 m |1877 |Destroyed; artifacts were recovered. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun''' |34°34′12.24″N 135°38′47.23″E |Empun |Diameter: 30 m |1877 |Destroyed; notable for cogwheel-shaped jade artifacts designated as Important Cultural Properties. |- |'''Nukudani West Kofun''' |34°34′14.08″N 135°38′48.82″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:atsuokayama Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60986182]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). !
