|— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

|— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60986182]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60986182]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60986182]] Wikidata name: Matsuokayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofuns located in [[:en:Kashiwara|Kashiwara, Osaka]], specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

|— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|WD=Q11618007|1=Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|WD=Q11530088|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|WD=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

|— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Matsuokayama Kofun Group]][[ja:松岳山古墳群]] [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}}|part_of={{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|qid=Q11618007|1=Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)}}; {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|qid=Q11530088|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}}}} {{Location map|Osaka city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=11.36|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=43.68|label=Matsuokayama
Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of Matsuokayama Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Matsuokayama Kofun Group''' (松岳山古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofuns located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, specifically in the Kokubu Ichiba area. One tumulus is designated as a National Historic Site, and several artifacts excavated from the group have been designated as Important Cultural Properties and Cultural Assets by Kashiwara City. == Overview == [[File:Matsuokayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Matsuokayama Kofun]]}}]] The Matsuokayama Kofun Group is situated on the Matsuokayama Hill, an alluvial terrace on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|qid=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} in eastern Osaka. It comprises at least nine mounds, including the central, large-scale [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] and smaller round or square mounds, though many have been destroyed due to residential development. The mounds extend from west to east along the hill and prominently use large quantities of andesite and basalt slabs for construction. Notably, the [[Matsuokayama Kofun]] is characterized by unique features such as slab construction, coffin shapes, standing stones, and elliptical cylindrical haniwa. These characteristics, combined with the rich funerary items from other mounds, make the group significant for understanding early Kofun-period society. Construction of the group is dated to the late 3rd to late 4th centuries CE, encompassing the early Kofun period. Alongside the contemporary {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, also in Kashiwara, it provides critical insights into the Yamato region before the establishment of the Furuichi Kofun Group, associated with the later Yamato kings. == Notable Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Matsuokayama Kofun Group Overview[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group] (Kashiwara City Website, updated October 25, 2016). ! Name !! Coordinates !! Shape !! Dimensions !! Survey !! |— |No records |Destroyed. |- |'''Nukudani East Kofun 2''' |34°34′14.17″N 135°38′50.95″E |Empun? |— |No records |Destroyed. |} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukaimachausuyama Kofun: ** Qinggai Banryu Mirror (1) ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors (2) * Cogwheel-shaped jade artifact from Nukudani Higashi-no-Otsuka Kofun. === National Historic Site === * Matsuokayama Kofun – Designated in 1922. === Kashiwara City Cultural Assets === * Artifacts from Matsuokayama Kofun: ** Elliptical cylindrical haniwa (3) ** Jar-shaped pottery (2) * Artifacts from Chausuyama Kofun: ** Triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror (1) ** Iron implements, stone tools, and haniwa. == Related Artifacts == * '''Bronze Inscription of the Ship Clan''': A National Treasure believed to have been unearthed from Matsuokayama, though details remain unclear. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016081400019/ Matsuokayama Kofun Group - Kashiwara City Website] {{translated page|ja|松岳山古墳群|version=72082786|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:松岳山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q60986182}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
